content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance * * Administration * * Enforcement * * Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-5444
                                                           322-5644

September 12, 1980

John F. Hann
Amador County Counsel
103 Court Street
Jackson, CA 95642

Dear Mr. Hann:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf
of Earl Withycombe. This advice is provided pursuant to
Government Code Section 83114. The facts as I understand
them from your letter of August 20, 1980, are as follows.

Mr. Withycombe is a member of the Board of Supervisors
of Sierra County. Sierra County, through the Sierra County
Air Pollution Control District, is a member of the Mountain
Counties Air Basin, but Mr. Withycombe does not represent
Sierra County on the Control Council, the governing body of
the Air Basin. In addition to his position as a supervisor
for Sierra County, Mr. Withycombe also serves as district
engineer for the Air Pollution Control District and as a
consulting engineer for the Air Basin. You have asked whether
it is permissible for Mr. Withycombe to hold these positions
while serving as a supervisor.

The prohibition against conflicts of interest contained
in the Political Reform Act of 1974 reads as follows:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his official position to influence
a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interest.

Government Code Section 87100

Mr. John F. Hahn
September 12, 1980
Page Two


The Political Reform Act therefore does not prohibit people from
holding public office because of conflict of interest; it in-
stead provides that once in office, they must refrain from
participating in certain decisions.1/

In addition, although a public official must generally
refrain from decisions which will have a material financial
effect on sources of income to them [Government Code Section
87103(c)], salary from a government agency does not constitute
"income" under the Political Reform Act. San Government Code
Section §2030(b)(2). Consequently, the Political Reform Act
will not require Mr. Whitcombe to disqualify himself from
decisions of the Sierra County Board of Supervisors simply
because those decisions will affect either the Air Basin or
the Pollution Control District. He would, however, be
required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions
of any of these agencies if those decisions would affect any
private clients he might have.

Finally, I can only advise you concerning the application
of the Political Reform Act. Other provisions of law such as
the prohibition against contractual conflicts of interest con-
tained in Government Code Section 1090 or the common law
doctrine of incompatibility of offices may have an application
in this situation.

If you have any further questions concerning the Political
Reform Act, please do not hesitate to call me.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Sarah T. Cameron
Deputy Chief for
Conflicts of Interest
Legal Division

STC:nrw


_________________________________________________________________

1/ Government Code Section 87103 contains the standards
for determining whether an official has a financial interest
in a decision.

AMADOR
COUNTY

COUNTY COUNSEL
108 COURT STREET

JOHN F. HAHN

JACKSON, CALIFORNIA 95642
TELEPHONE (209) 223-1818

August 20, 1980


Mr. Ted Prim, Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission

Dear Mr. Prim:

The Mountain Counties Air Basin is a regional air basin
established pursuant to Part 1 of Division 26 of the California Health and Safety Code. The control council, which
is the governing board of the Mountain Counties Air Basin,
has asked me to request an opinion from you as to whether or
not the Basin, through its control council, may contract
with Mr. Earl Withycombe for the latter's furnishing of air
pollution consultant services to the Air Basin. The problem
giving rise for the need for the opinion is that Mr. Withycombe
is a member of the board of supervisors of Sierra County,
which County is a member of the Basin. I have been asked to
write to you to request an opinion as to whether such a
contract creates a conflict of interest so that Mr. Withycombe
would be prevented from entering into the contract. Please
note that Mr. Withycombe, should he enter into the contract,
would not participate in any decision of the board of supervisors of Sierra County as a supervisor on any matter relating to air pollution.

I am enclosing a letter from Mr. Withycombe to me dated
December 9, 1979, and copies of relevant contracts, agreements, bylaws, conflict of interest codes, and disclosure
statements which accompanied the letter.

Please note that Mr. Withycombe does not represent Sierra
County on the council. It may be of further interest to you
that Mr. Withycombe has two positions: one as the district
engineer for the air pollution control district for Sierra
County and as the consultant for the Basin; he held both of
those positions prior to his being elected to Sierra County's
board of supervisors. Both Mr. Withycombe and the council
wish to be assured that his holding of the two positions is
not unlawful.

It is my opinion that there is no inherent conflict of
interest between Mr. Withycombe's acting as the engineer for
the Basin (and the district) while serving as a member of
Sierra County's board of supervisors provided that does
not participate as a supervisor in any matter relating to
air pollution, the district, and the Basin which come before
the Sierra County board of supervisors, and as long as he
does not represent Sierra County on the Basin's council.

Please let me know if you disagree with my opinion.

Yours very truly,

[signature]

JOHN F. HAHN
County Counsel

JFH/p

Enc: a/s


- 2 -

sections.question: Whether it is permissible for Mr. Earl Withycombe to hold the positions of Sierra County Board of Supervisors member, district engineer for the Air Pollution Control District, and consulting engineer for the Air Basin simultaneously.
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit people from holding public office because of conflict of interest; it instead provides that once in office they must refrain from participating in certain decisions. Mr. Withycombe would not be required to disqualify himself from decisions of the Sierra County Board of Supervisors simply because those decisions would affect either the Air Basin or the Air Pollution Control District, since salary from a government agency does not constitute 'income' under the Political Reform Act. However, he would be required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions of any of these agencies if those decisions would affect any private clients he might have.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a county supervisor simultaneously serve as district engineer for an air pollution control district and consulting engineer for a regional air basin when the county is a member of that basin?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under the Political Reform Act, Mr. Withycombe may hold all three positions provided he disqualifies himself from Sierra County Board of Supervisors decisions affecting the Air Basin or Air Pollution Control District, and also disqualifies himself from any agency decisions affecting private clients. The FPPC notes that other laws such as Government Code Section 1090 or common law incompatibility doctrines may also apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (August 20, 1980) and FPPC response letter (September 12, 1980). Incoming request letter contains County Counsel's preliminary opinion agreeing with the FPPC's later conclusion. Question and conclusion sections identified from FPPC response by Sarah T. Cameron, Deputy Chief for Conflicts of Interest.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether it is permissible for Mr. Earl Withycombe to hold the positions of Sierra County Board of Supervisors member, district engineer for the Air Pollution Control District, and consulting engineer for the Air Basin simultaneously.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit people from holding public office because of conflict of interest; it instead provides that once in office they must refrain from participating in certain decisions. Mr. Withycombe would not be required to disqualify himself from decisions of the Sierra County Board of Supervisors simply because those decisions would affect either the Air Basin or the Air Pollution Control District, since salary from a government agency does not constitute 'income' under the Political Reform Act. However, he would be required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions of any of these agencies if those decisions would affect any private clients he might have.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Amador County Counsel's request regarding whether Earl Withycombe, a Sierra County Board of Supervisors member, may simultaneously serve as district engineer for the Air Pollution Control District and consulting engineer for the Mountain Counties Air Basin without violating conflict of interest laws.
